name: EMU (Extravehicular Mobility Unit)
best_known_for: The white US spacewalk suit that has flown every American EVA from the Shuttle through the ISS
credit: Modular EVA suit with a hard upper torso, soft fabric arms and legs, and a backpack-mounted Primary Life Support System. Operates at 4.3 psi pure O₂. Originally fielded for Shuttle EVAs (1981), still in service on the ISS. SAFER mini-thruster pack added for tether-loss rescue.
